revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24271391609|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上巳節会]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上巳節会]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上巳節会]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上巳節会]]
{{Lunisolar March}} '''Jōshisetsukai''' (上巳節会) is a feast held on {{ill|March 3 (Lunar Calendar)|ja|3月3日 (旧暦)|lt=March 3|lt_ja=3月3日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 3 (Lunar Calendar)|1=March 3 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q839268}} in ancient Japan, during which the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}} distributed offerings.
== Overview == The original definition of '''{{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|qid=Q698091|lt=Jōshi|1=Double Third Festival}}''' is the first day of {{ill|March (Lunar Calendar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March|lt_ja=3月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunar Calendar)|1=March (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q838927}} that falls on {{ill|Snake (zodiac)|ja|巳|1=Snake_(zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|lt=Snake Day|lt_ja=巳の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16676479}}, and because in China during the {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|qid=Q320930|lt=Wei|1=Cao Wei}} period this day was fixed as March 3, it became established in the Muromachi period as the "March 3 Ceremony."
In China, it was customary on this day to perform rituals of {{ill|Harae|en|Harae|qid=Q3127244|lt=Harae|1=Harae}} or {{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|qid=Q1936265|lt=misogi|1=Misogi}}, and in Japan, under the influence of {{ill|Miscellaneous Edicts|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Edicts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the feast was officially adopted from the first year of the {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|qid=Q1346269|lt=Taibō|1=Taihō (era)}} era ({{ill|701|en|701|qid=Q23496|lt=701|1=701}}), with the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|内裏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Palace|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3012148}} hosting the {{ill|Winding stream party|en|Winding stream party|qid=Q5360526|lt=Banquet by the Winding Stream|1=Winding stream party}}. Although the ceremony was abolished during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heijō|1=Emperor Heizei}} because it was near the national memorial of his father, {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kammu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, his brother, {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, revived it not as an official feast but as a less formal ceremony. Furthermore, the Banquet by the Winding Stream was also held as aristocratic entertainment, with records in the {{ill|Record of the Imperial Regent of the Mido|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Record of the Imperial Regent of the Mido|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488903}} noting that even {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|qid=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} held one in his private residence. In addition, among the common people, a tradition developed in which one would rub one's body against dolls ({{ill|Doll|en|Doll|qid=Q168658|lt=human figures|1=Doll}}) known as "Agamono" to transfer impurities ({{ill|kegare|ja|穢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}), and then cast them into rivers or other bodies of water for purification; this practice, associated with the figures known as Amakatsu/Tenji (あまかつ) and Hōko (ほうこ), is said to have evolved into the later tradition of {{ill|Nagashi-bina|ja|流し雛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashi-bina|1=nagashibina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5371831}} ({{ill|Hina Doll|ja|雛人形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hina Doll|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 雛祭り|1=Hinamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q844110}}).
== References == * Yamanaka Yū, "Jōshi" (in ''Kokushi Dai Jiten 7'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1986, ISBN 978-4-642-00507-4) * {{ill|Takei Akio|ja|竹居明男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takei Akio|1=Akio Takei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24576764}}, "The Jōshi Feast" (in ''Nihon Kodai-shi Dai Jiten'', Yamato Shobō, 2006, ISBN 4-479-84065-6)
== See Also == * {{ill|Hinamatsuri|ja|ひな祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinamatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 雛祭り|1=Hinamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q844110}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{citation needed}} {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} {{translated page|ja|上巳節会|version=90664039|comment=Imported full ja history}}
